title: Would a Massachusetts income tax cut actually help you? Here’s what to know.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts considers lowering the state income tax rate from 5% to 4%, potentially impacting state revenue significantly.
Median households may save approximately $1,250 annually, with larger benefits for higher-income groups, according to Tufts University analysis.
The proposal could reduce annual state revenue by about $5.1 billion, raising concerns about funding for essential services.

title: 3 ways Cade Cunningham’s absence could impact the Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cade Cunningham is out with a collapsed lung and will be re-evaluated in two weeks by the Detroit Pistons.
Cunningham's absence may affect the Celtics' playoff path as they are 3.5 games behind first-seeded Detroit.
Cunningham's injury could shift Jaylen Brown's MVP race position if Cunningham becomes ineligible due to missed games.

title: Readers say this is Massachusetts’ most stressful commute — and it’s not I-93 North
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston.com readers identify the Southeast Expressway as Massachusetts' most stressful commute, surpassing the Zakim Bridge/I-93 North.
Nearly 50% of surveyed readers highlighted the frequent congestion on the Southeast Expressway, calling it outdated and perpetually jammed.
I-95/Route 128 in Waltham received 18% of votes, cited for its tight traffic and unpredictable slowdowns.

title: A grave situation: Medway police seek return of 12-foot-tall skeleton’s head
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Kevin Skellington, a 12-foot-tall plastic skeleton's head, went missing from Medway Recreation Center over the weekend.
The disappearance of the skeleton head is being investigated as larceny, with police reviewing security footage.
Medway Police ask residents for tips on locating the head, stating it's too heavy to have moved far easily.

title: Dropkick Murphys to play at ‘No Kings’ rally on Boston Common
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dropkick Murphys will perform at the 'No Kings' rally on Boston Common on March 28 from 2 to 4 p.m.
The rally, part of a nationwide 'No Kings Day' of action, is organized by ACLU Massachusetts and other groups.
The event coincides with Dropkick Murphys' tour and aims to protest injustices under the Trump administration.

title: Sold out show? Not if you book at this Boston hotel.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Omni Boston Hotel at the Seaport offers All Access Passes with VIP tickets to sold-out concerts and events.
Pass packages range from $1,000 to $1,300 and include premium features like transportation and in-room amenities.
Events include Lady Gaga, Ariana Grande concerts, Broadway shows, World Cup games, and Boston sports playoffs.

title: Six from N.H. charged in ‘brazen’ Downtown Crossing assault and robbery
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Six New Hampshire residents charged after a 19-year-old was attacked and robbed at Downtown Crossing MBTA station.
Suspects include Luiz Loaiza, Jose Soto, Cameroon Castro, Anthony Perry, Alexander Loaiza, and Giavanna Williams.
Judge set bail at $500 for each and ordered them to avoid contact with the victim until May court hearing.

title: Review: ‘Project Hail Mary’ borders on miraculous
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Project Hail Mary' stars Ryan Gosling as an amnesiac astronaut on a quest to save Earth from extinction.
The film, directed by Phil Lord and Christopher Miller, blends sci-fi with comedy, featuring a unique alien companion.
'Project Hail Mary' is entertaining but criticized for an overlong runtime and a cumbersome ending.

title: City Council calls for more transparency from Wu over transportation projects
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council seeks more transparency from Mayor Wu on stalled transportation projects requiring her personal approval.
Councilor Liz Breadon highlights that $200 million in transportation investments could be at risk due to project delays.
Councilor Sharon Durkan calls for a comprehensive update on Boston's transportation goals, pending since 'Go Boston 2030.'

title: ‘All the negativity that’s in this town sucks’: Revisiting Rick Pitino’s iconic Celtics rant
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Rick Pitino's iconic 2000 Celtics rant highlighted his frustration with the team's struggles and the Boston sports atmosphere.
Pitino criticized fan expectations, mentioning past Celtics legends like Larry Bird, Kevin McHale, and Robert Parish.
Pitino coached the Celtics from 1997 to 2001, with a record of 102-146, resigning after three-and-a-half seasons.
